opportunities.title: Killer Clowns as a Shadowdark Monster: Statting the 'Send in the Clowns' Trope
opportunities.why_now: EN World just ran a piece on the evil-clown trope in RPGs, giving a ready-made hook to convert into an actual stat block.
opportunities.angle: 60-sec creature tactic short: stat out a homebrew clown monster (fear aura, disadvantage on saves vs. laughter) for Shadowdark, with one paragraph DCC funnel variant

opportunities.title: Mike Shea Went on the D&D News Livestream — Here's the One Ruling Worth Stealing
opportunities.why_now: Podcast #405 dropped with Sly Flourish's Mike Shea as guest, a name your audience already trusts for table-ready advice.
opportunities.angle: Talking-head short: pull a single concrete takeaway from the livestream (not a recap) and give your own dry veteran-GM verdict on whether it's actually usable at the table

opportunities.title: This Week's D&D News Digest: What Actually Changes Your Session Zero
opportunities.why_now: EN World's weekly digest is a grab-bag of announcements; most GMs won't read it, so a filtered 'what matters' short earns trust.
opportunities.angle: Rapid-fire 3-bullet short: strip the digest down to the one or two items with real table impact, skip the rest with a dismissive one-liner

opportunities.title: Sly Flourish's 'Lazy Campaign' Method: The One Prep Step You Can Cut Tonight
opportunities.why_now: Sly Flourish just published a fresh breakdown of lazy campaign prep, a direct match for the channel's efficiency-obsessed GM voice.
opportunities.angle: Rules-ruling/prep short: take one specific lazy-prep technique from the article (e.g., skip the flowchart, prep NPC motivations not plots) and show it applied to tonight's session

opportunities.title: Perils of the Warp, DCC-Style: Stealing Owlcat's New Psyker for Your Corruption Table
opportunities.why_now: Owlcat just revealed a psyker companion for Warhammer 40K: Dark Heresy, a built-in excuse to talk psychic-mishap mechanics that map directly onto DCC corruption and Shadowdark mystic knacks.
opportunities.angle: Spell use-case short: compare 40k's Perils of the Warp risk/reward to a DCC corruption roll, propose a homebrew mishap table GMs can drop in immediately
